highlights.item: Phase 07 is now explicit so the first-party CRM rebuild is tracked as its own numbered delivery stream instead of being hidden across phases 05 and 06.
A capability map now translates HubSpot primitives into first-party Payload collections, Next.js internal APIs, Stripe webhook reconciliation, and Payload-jobs-driven Resend sequences.

The CRM foundation schema now exists in repo with `accounts`, `contacts`, `leads`, `opportunities`, `crm-activities`, `crm-tasks`, `crm-sequences`, and `sequence-enrollments`, including commercial account fields and an in-app sequence-definition model.
Public form submissions and quote lifecycle hooks now create or update first-party CRM records through small internal service files instead of leaving the sync layer as a no-op placeholder.

`07-02` now has a real server-side workspace layer: `src/lib/crm/workspace/*` plus admin-only routes at `src/app/api/internal/crm/workspace/route.ts` and `src/app/api/internal/crm/record/route.ts` for queue summaries and list/detail reads.

`07-03` now has a compact CRM tab inside `/ops`, built from smaller command-center components and a dedicated `src/components/portal/crm/CrmWorkspace.tsx` surface that keeps details in the left rail instead of opening overlays.
Focused validation also started under `07-06`: CRM workspace integration tests plus Playwright admin/frontend coverage now pass alongside typecheck and production build.
The CRM workspace now supports server-backed search plus inline quick actions for task status updates and opportunity stage advancement via small admin-only mutation routes.

The left detail rail is now structured for CRM records instead of flattening everything into plain text, with compact tabs, richer company fields, and inline internal note logging backed by `crm-activities`.

`07-02` is now complete for the current scope: the CRM API/query layer supports queue/detail reads, search, stale detection, task and opportunity actions, owner reassignment, lead qualification/disqualification, and owner/commercial filtering through focused admin-only routes.

`07-03` is now complete for the current scope: the CRM workspace uses compact list/detail cards with status badges, stale indicators, quick actions, owner and commercial filters, and a left-rail detail view without reverting to overlapping overlays.

`07-04` billing planning is now concrete: Stripe-hosted invoices and portal-first self-service are the default customer-facing payment UX for account holders, while Payload remains the operational billing ledger and must support commercial net-30 monthly invoicing plus onsite/out-of-band payments, discounts, credits, and refunds.

Company-account customer scope is now explicit instead of inferred: `users` carry an `account` relationship, quotes/invoices/service plans/appointments carry the same account reference, those records auto-resolve their account when possible, and an admin preview toolbar can impersonate customer users across the frontend and portal without exposing `/ops` or admin docs to the effective non-admin user.
Phase planning now includes an explicit operating-rhythm workflow with multi-role employee duties, queue/SLA expectations, and the required next-action contract so daily ownership is not implicit.
Reminder strategy is now explicit: fastest rollout uses Payload jobs + Resend to each worker's preferred existing inbox (personal or company), while Gmail/Microsoft OAuth remains optional later work.

Boundary policy is now reinforced in planning as a first-class requirement: cross-user/company views and internal docs remain staff-only; customers remain self/account-scoped, with company-admin customers limited to their own company user management.

The operating-rhythm workflow now includes tighter v1 SLA proposals (lead/quote/schedule/invoice), explicit support/refund SLA classes, and a task-first role model suited to a two-person startup team.
Discount policy planning is now explicit: support both account-wide defaults and user-level overrides, with precedence and audit behavior tracked as a dedicated open billing decision/task.

Ops IA planning now requires a route split for dense modules and a conventional shadcn app shell: command center becomes current-user duties, while CRM/scorecard/milestones/assets/today board move to dedicated `/ops/*` pages.
Portal shell hardening is now tracked to prevent sidebar/content overlap, horizontal overflow, and hidden impersonation toolbar states.

A new engineering-conventions track now exists for quality guardrails: icon-forward form/action affordances, reduced copy-heavy faded UI patterns, centralized literals via typed shared constants, and extraction triggers for large files/reusable utilities.
Conventions are now measurable and pattern-backed: extraction thresholds (repeat counts and size guardrails) plus a shared catalog of preferred app patterns for ops/customer implementations.
A refactor extraction roadmap now anchors the target folder structure and prioritized split queue so architecture cleanup can happen incrementally alongside feature delivery.

Portal nav context is now staff-route aware: real admins see customer-account chrome on `/dashboard` and staff chrome on `/ops` and `/docs`, with `(admin)` / `(test_user)` labels and a seeded `test_user@grimetime.local` customer for impersonation checks (`07-13` closed).

`/ops` now has both a dashboard hub and real focused `/ops/*` pages for CRM, today board, scorecard, milestones, and assets. The old redirect-only deep links were replaced by shared route loaders plus focused list/detail shells, while the main ops dashboard still accepts `?tab=` for the command-center hub.

Portal shell refactoring has started in earnest: the sidebar now uses shared nav builders, the layout uses an extracted `PortalAppShell`, and the shell CSS is closer to conventional shadcn sidebar/inset behavior with less horizontal-overflow risk.

Refactor-roadmap execution now includes shared ops UI metadata plus typed `/ops` query utilities (`parseOpsTabQuery` / `buildOpsTabUrl`) instead of leaving tab IDs and route behavior scattered across components.

Phase 07 now explicitly treats `/ops` as the duties-first staff dashboard and the focused `/ops/*` pages as the places where dense interaction happens, rather than trying to keep full work surfaces embedded inside one hub.

Phase 07 now also has an explicit client-data rule: server components own first load, while TanStack Query backs live mutable portal and ops surfaces such as CRM, billing, invites, and account updates.

`07-04` is now implemented end-to-end for the current scope: Stripe-backed billing routes, webhook reconciliation, customer billing portal access, hosted invoice flows, service-plan sync helpers, monthly consolidated invoice creation, billing-event audit entries, and compact admin billing actions all exist in the app.

`07-05` is now live for the current scope: Payload jobs run customer notifications and sequence steps through a cron-driven job endpoint, default CRM sequences are seeded, overdue invoice scans create dunning work, and quote/invoice/appointment lifecycle emails can embed portal claim/invite CTAs when the recipient has not activated access yet.

`07-08` is now enforced for the current scope: effective-user auth plus account/company guards keep customer views self/account-scoped, company-admin users limited to their own company membership, and staff-only docs/ops routes hidden during customer preview or non-admin use.

`07-12` is now closed for the current scope: `/ops` functions as the duties-first staff dashboard, while dense CRM, today-board, scorecard, milestones, and assets work happens in focused `/ops/*` routes backed by shared loaders and query helpers instead of one overloaded command-center page.

`07-09` is now closed for the current scope: `npm run verify:mcp` validates Cursor/Codex config stubs, Payload MCP endpoint reachability and auth acceptance, and image-gen stdio startup so local MCP readiness is testable instead of implicit.

`07-06` gained broader billing/customer regression coverage: the billing workspace now has focused integration coverage, customer portal billing summary assertions are explicit, and the admin impersonation browser flow verifies the customer billing-management card for company accounts.

`07-11` is now closed for the current scope: portal shell layout variables are centralized, the admin preview toolbar uses the shared shell offset, sticky ops rails respect a shared portal top offset, and focused `/ops/*` pages no longer stack a second section header card on top of the main page header.

`07-14` and `07-15` both moved forward again: portal shell dimensions now live in `src/lib/portal/layout.ts`, and repeated ops-section rail content was extracted into `src/components/portal/ops/OpsSectionCardContent.tsx` instead of being duplicated across the command-center and focused-route shells.

`07-01` is now complete for the current scope: CRM owner fallback is deterministic, support/refund/billing/policy requests map into shared task-policy builders, and `crm-tasks` now carry typed source, SLA, role-tag, next-action, and escalation metadata instead of ad hoc strings.

`07-10` is now complete for the current scope: account-wide discount defaults and user-level overrides are implemented with explicit precedence, surfaced in billing workspace UI, and seeded into demo data so billing adjustments are explainable during ops review.

`07-06` is now complete for the current scope: unit tests cover operating-rhythm and discount-policy logic, integration coverage exercises CRM/billing workspace behavior, Playwright passes against the built app, and demo seed data includes commercial billing and discount scenarios.

`07-07` is now complete for the current scope: the role matrix, SLA classes, queue priorities, escalation behavior, and refund/policy routing now live in shared operating-rhythm/task-policy modules and show up in `/ops` duty guidance.

`07-14` and `07-15` are now complete for the current scope: repeated literals moved into typed policy/meta modules, billing UI was split into smaller focused components, and shared policy/data builders now handle CRM task creation and billing discount behavior instead of embedding those rules inside larger UI or route files.

Phase 07 is now complete for the current roadmap scope: the repo has a first-party CRM workspace, Stripe-backed billing sync and controls, Payload-jobs-driven sequences, deterministic impersonation-safe boundaries, route-based `/ops` IA, operational MCP verification, demo data, and the refactor/conventions baseline needed before phase 08 AI work.

next.item: Phase 07 is closed. The next roadmap work before AI is phase 03 marketing/media completion and any post-close maintenance or regression fixes on CRM, billing, and automation surfaces.

When phase 08 starts, reuse the now-stable phase 07 boundaries: first-party CRM data stays the system of record, customer/admin scopes remain strict, and any assistant tooling must sit on top of the existing ops/billing/query modules instead of bypassing them.
